rationales.hook_strength: 'Dark chocolate for a sharper brain' is concrete and has a feel-good payoff, but the mechanism sub-line is dense for a cover — slightly overwrites the hook.
rationales.clarity: Every slide is one idea with a clear label, logical progression from compound → mechanism → payoff → how-to → dose, nothing to re-read.
rationales.audience_fit: 'Tired by 3pm' audience will latch onto focus/memory angle, and the '≥70%, one square' instruction is low-effort to act on — good fit, though not urgency-driven.
rationales.novelty: Dark chocolate brain benefits are a well-worn content category; the nitric-oxide framing adds a layer but won't feel fresh to anyone who follows health accounts.
rationales.brand_fit: Claims are properly hedged ('linked to', 'is linked to'), no hypey words, the CTA ties directly to the Health Score feature without AI-cringe.
rationales.predicted_performance: Saves likely on the dose/choice slides, but the topic is saturated enough that shares will be modest unless the hook thumbnail leans harder into the chocolate visual.
